title: Married women are required by law to obey their husbands
subtitle: Any provision stating that married women must obey their husband or if disobeying their husband has legal ramifications for them, such as loss of their right to maintenance.
caption:
Married women are required by law to obey their husbands

Any provision stating that married women must obey their husband or if disobeying their husband has legal ramifications for them, such as loss of their right to maintenance.

This choropleth world map displays the legal status of married women regarding obedience to their husbands in 2023. The legend distinguishes between three categories: countries where such a provision exists ("Yes"), countries where it does not ("No"), and areas with no available data. The "Yes" category is represented by an orange color, while the "No" category is shown in purple. A distinct cluster of countries in North Africa and the Middle East, including Morocco, Algeria, Tunisia, Libya, Egypt, Sudan, Saudi Arabia, Yemen, Oman, and the United Arab Emirates, are colored orange, indicating the presence of laws requiring wives to obey their husbands. Additionally, Malaysia and Indonesia in Southeast Asia also fall into this "Yes" category. The vast majority of the world's countries, including all of North and South America, Europe, most of Sub-Saharan Africa, Russia, China, India, and Australia, are colored purple, signifying that no such legal provision exists. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, indicating that no data is available for this territory. The visual pattern shows that legal requirements for wifely obedience are concentrated in a specific region spanning North Africa through the Middle East and parts of Southeast Asia, while the rest of the globe largely lacks such statutes.
